publications.title: Spectral Disentanglement: Rank-Aware Task Adaptation for Rehearsal-free Continual Learning in LLMs
publications.abstract: SpaRTA tackles rehearsal-free continual learning in LLMs with rank-aware spectral disentanglement that separates shared knowledge from task-specific adaptation. It mitigates catastrophic forgetting while preserving efficient transfer across evolving tasks.

publications.title: SparK: Query-Aware Unstructured Sparsity with Recoverable KV Cache Channel Pruning
publications.abstract: SparK compresses long-context KV caches with query-aware unstructured channel sparsity and a recoverable pruning scheme. It reduces memory and attention cost while preserving answer quality under long-context inference.

publications.title: Neural-Symbolic Collaborative Distillation: Advancing Small Language Models for Complex Reasoning Tasks
publications.abstract: NesyCD distills complex reasoning into small language models by coupling neural learning with symbolic knowledge transfer. It strengthens specialized reasoning skills that are difficult to capture from neural supervision alone.

publications.title: SKIntern: Internalizing Symbolic Knowledge for Distilling Better CoT Capabilities into Small Language Models
publications.abstract: SKIntern internalizes symbolic knowledge during CoT distillation so small language models learn stronger reasoning traces instead of only imitating outputs. It improves compact models on reasoning tasks with more effective distillation.

publications.title: AAG: Learning to Awaken Internal Knowledge of Large Language Models for Question Answering
publications.abstract: AAG awakens LLMs' internal knowledge for question answering without relying on external retrieval at inference time. It improves factual QA while avoiding the extra context cost and noise sensitivity of document-based augmentation.

publications.title: From Instance Training to Instruction Learning: Task Adapters Generation from Instructions
publications.abstract: TAGI generates task adapters directly from natural-language instructions, shifting adaptation from instance-heavy training toward instruction-driven task learning. It improves task generalization when labeled examples are scarce.

publications.title: Dynamic Weighted Neural Bellman-Ford Network for Knowledge Graph Reasoning
publications.abstract: Dynamic Weighted Neural Bellman-Ford Network models relation importance dynamically for different entities and reasoning paths in knowledge graphs. It improves multi-hop reasoning with path-aware message propagation instead of static relation weighting.

preprints.title: ResAdapt: Adaptive Resolution for Efficient Multimodal Reasoning
preprints.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve stronger visual understanding by scaling input fidelity, yet the resulting visual token growth makes jointly sustaining high spatial resolution and long temporal context prohibitive. We argue that the bottleneck lies not in how post-encoding representations are compressed but in the volume of pixels the encoder receives, and address it with ResAdapt, an Input-side adaptation framework that learns how much visual budget each frame should receive before encoding. ResAdapt couples a lightweight Allocator with an unchanged MLLM backbone, so the backbone retains its native visual-token interface while receiving an operator-transformed input. We formulate allocation as a contextual bandit and train the Allocator with Cost-Aware Policy Optimization (CAPO), which converts sparse rollout feedback into a stable accuracy-cost learning signal. Across budget-controlled video QA, temporal grounding, and image reasoning tasks, ResAdapt improves low-budget operating points and often lies on or near the efficiency-accuracy frontier, with the clearest gains on reasoning-intensive benchmarks under aggressive compression. Notably, ResAdapt supports up to 16x more frames at the same visual budget while delivering over 15% performance gain.

preprints.title: Beyond Hard and Soft: Hybrid Context Compression for Balancing Local and Global Information Retention
preprints.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) encounter significant challenges in long-sequence inference due to computational inefficiency and redundant processing, driving interest in context compression techniques.

collaborations.title: Harmonizing the Past, Present, and Future: A Null-Space Constrained Region-Specific Method for Continual Learning in LLMs
collaborations.abstract: PaRSP harmonizes stability, plasticity, and future capacity in continual LLM adaptation by learning sparse task-specific parameter regions under null-space constraints. It preserves long-term knowledge while sustaining future adaptation, achieving strong results on standard continual learning and long-sequence benchmarks.

collaborations.title: Pushing the Limits of LLM Tool Calling via Experiential Knowledge Integration and Activation
collaborations.abstract: This work studies how experiential knowledge acquisition, activation, and internalization affect multi-step LLM tool use. Based on these findings, KATE combines experiential knowledge with width-expanded inference and knowledge-aware training, improving BFCL-V3 and AppWorld across model scales.

collaborations.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It
collaborations.abstract: Tool use enables large language models to perform complex tasks, but reinforcement learning alone can be unstable in multi-step tool-use settings. This work analyzes collapse patterns caused by probability spikes in control tokens and studies supervisory signals that stabilize tool-use RL.

collaborations.title: Bottom-up Policy Optimization: Your Language Model Policy Secretly Contains Internal Policies
collaborations.abstract: Bottom-up Policy Optimization decomposes an LLM policy into internal layer and modular policies, then optimizes lower layers to reconstruct the reasoning foundation from the bottom up for stronger complex-reasoning performance.

collaborations.title: A Comprehensive Survey on Long Context Language Modeling
collaborations.abstract: Efficient processing of long contexts has been a persistent pursuit in Natural Language Processing. With the growing number of long documents, dialogues, and other textual data, it is important to develop Long Context Language Models (LCLMs) that can process and analyze extensive inputs in an effective and efficient way.

collaborations.title: Evaluating Personalized Tool-Augmented LLMs from the Perspectives of Personalization and Proactivity
collaborations.abstract: ETAPP evaluates personalized tool-augmented LLMs along both personalization and proactivity, providing a benchmark for user-aligned tool use. It exposes gaps missed by evaluations that focus on only one of the two dimensions.

collaborations.title: CITI: Enhancing Tool Utilizing Ability in Large Language Models without Sacrificing General Performance
collaborations.abstract: CITI improves tool-use ability in large language models without sacrificing their general capabilities. It balances tool specialization and general performance with an efficient adaptation strategy.

collaborations.title: Better wit than wealth: Dynamic Parametric Retrieval Augmented Generation for Test-time Knowledge Enhancement
collaborations.abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) enhances large language models (LLMs) by retrieving relevant documents from external sources and incorporating them into the context. While it improves reliability by providing factual texts, it significantly increases inference costs as context length grows.

collaborations.title: Shuttle Between Symbolic Instructions and Neural Parameters of Large Language Models
collaborations.abstract: SHIP shuttles between symbolic instructions and neural parameters, unifying instruction construction with parameter adaptation for LLMs. It improves task transfer by bridging symbolic task descriptions and model-side learning.

collaborations.title: LMTuner: An user-friendly and highly-integrable Training Framework for fine-tuning Large Language Models
collaborations.abstract: With the burgeoning development in the realm of large language models (LLMs), the demand for efficient incremental training tailored to specific industries and domains continues to increase. Currently, the predominantly employed frameworks lack modular design, it often takes a lot of coding work to kickstart the training of LLM.
